paper_title: A Roadmap for Investment Promotion and Export Diversification: The Case of Jordan
research_answer: Jordan experienced a prolonged and severe economic deterioration: nine consecutive years (2008–2017) of negative GDP per capita growth, resulting in a cumulative loss of 14% in GDP per capita over the decade from 2009 to 2018. This makes Jordan's case for diversification particularly urgent. Its economic complexity is already lower than what its income level would predict — itself a warning signal for slower future growth — and its export base is less diversified than benchmark peers, with industries that tend to be more common and less sophisticated globally. The combination of a shrinking economic base and a relatively weak productive structure underscores why broadening and upgrading exports is critical.
supporting_quotes: Failure to cope with a 50.4% growth in population led to nine consecutive years (2008-2017) of negative growth rates in GDP per capita, resulting in a cumulative loss of 14.0% over the past decade (2009-2018).
Jordan shows an ECI index that is lower than what can be expected for its income per capita level.
Jordan, with a diversity of 178, ranks second-to-last among the benchmark countries.
